[Sun 2026-04-05 15:51 UTC] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: # ULTRAPLAN: D&D Paul Simulator — TOTAL UI/UX REMODEL + AI Assistant

You have up to 20 minutes. Do NOT execute. Produce a comprehensive plan for 4 agents.

## CORE PHILOSOPHY: K.I.S.S. (Keep It Simple Stupid)
Lucy's exact words: "aku ingin orang bodoh pun paham pake aplikasi ini"
- NO complex menus. NO hidden features. Everything visible and obvious.
- ONE app, ALL features. No separate pages (guide.html, flow.html) — everything in index.html.
- Big buttons, clear labels, visual feedback on every action.
- Mobile-first (most D&D players use phones at the table).

## Current State

### Frontend
- `index.html` (1852 lines) — main app
- `guide.html` (1064 lines) — separate D&D guide page
- `flow.html` (366 lines) — separate flow view
- `styles.css` (6130 lines) — massive CSS file

### Backend
- `server.js` (2600 lines) — WebSocket + REST API
- `bestiary-data.js` (74 lines) — monster data
- `spells-data.js` (140 lines) — spell data

### Current Features (from ID audit)
- PIN-based auth (6-digit DM PIN, 4-digit player PIN)
- Room creation (DM creates, players join)
- Sidebar nav: Party, Manage, Character, Dice, Initiative, Spells, NPCs, Monsters, Map
- Character sheets
- Dice roller
- Initiative tracker
- Spell lookup
- NPC/Monster reference
- Session summary (AI-generated via Bedrock)
- Save to Obsidian
- Session history

### Backend API
- Room CRUD (create, join, auth)
- Character CRUD
- Initiative tracking
- Notes system
- AI summary generation (POST /api/rooms/:pin/summary — uses Bedrock)
- Spell/Monster lookup
- Session save/end/history

## What Lucy Wants

### 1. ALL-IN-ONE APP
No more separate pages. Everything in ONE page:
- Guide content → integrated as a help panel/tooltip system
- Flow view → integrated into the main app
- No page switches. Ever.

### 2. AI ASSISTANT (THE BIG NEW FEATURE)
A chat panel where both DM and Players can ask AI questions:
- "What happens if I cast Fireball in a 10ft room?"
- "Suggest a random encounter for a level 5 party in a forest"
- "What's the DC for climbing a wet cliff?"
- "Help me roleplay this tavern keeper NPC"
- "What are my options in this situation?" (contextual based on gameplay)

The AI should have CONTEXT of the current session:
- Current party members + their stats
- Initiative order
- Recent dice rolls
- Session notes
- Current combat state

Backend: Use existing Bedrock integration (already used for session summaries). New endpoint: `POST /api/rooms/:pin/ai-chat` that includes session context.

### 3. SIMPLIFIED UI — K.I.S.S. LAYOUT

**Current problem:** 9 sidebar nav items is overwhelming. Most are rarely used.

**New layout (mobile-first):**

**Bottom tab bar (4 tabs max):**
1. 🎲 **Play** — dice roller + initiative + combat (the stuff you use every turn)
2. 📋 **Party** — character sheets + party overview
3. 🤖 **AI** — AI assistant chat
4. ☰ **More** — everything else (spells, monsters, NPCs, map, settings, guide)

**Why 4 tabs:** Thumb-friendly on mobile. No scrolling through nav items. 4 is the maximum before it gets confusing.

**Play tab (most used):**
- BIG dice buttons (d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20) — tap to roll, shows result with animation
- Dice roll history (scrolling)
- Initiative tracker (if combat active)
- Quick actions: "Roll Attack", "Roll Save", "Roll Skill Check"

**Party tab:**
- List of all party members with HP bars, AC, level
- Tap a character → full character sheet
- DM can edit characters
- Players can only edit their own

**AI tab (NEW):**
- Chat interface (like MAGI chat but simpler)
- AI has session context (party, combat, notes)
- Suggested prompts: "Random encounter", "NPC idea", "Rule check", "What should I do?"
- History persists within the session

**More tab:**
- Grid of feature cards: Spells 📖, Monsters 🐉, NPCs 👤, Map 🗺️, Notes 📝, Guide 📚, History 📜, Settings ⚙️
- Each opens as a full-screen panel that slides in (not a new page)

### 4. VISUAL DESIGN — Apex Dark + Amber

Already partially done (from earlier Apex overhaul). Refine:
- Dark base: #0d121b
- Amber/gold accent: #d4a73a (NOT cyan — this is D&D, not a dev tool)
- JetBrains Mono for stats/numbers
- Cinzel (serif) for headings/titles (fantasy feel)
- Grid texture background
- Card-based UI with minimal borders
- Dice roll results: BIG number, centered, with subtle animation
- HP bars: green → yellow → red gradient

### 5. ONBOARDING — Zero Confusion

Current PIN system is confusing. Simplify:

**DM flow:**
1. "Create Game" → big button
2. Enter your name → creates room → shows PIN
3. "Share this PIN with your players" → copy button
4. Add players or wait for them to join

**Player flow:**
1. "Join Game" → big button  
2. Enter PIN + your name → you're in
3. See your character sheet immediately

**Solo flow:**
1. "Solo Adventure" → big button
2. AI becomes your DM
3. Full access to all features

## Read These Files
- ~/dnd-app/frontend/index.html (current main app)
- ~/dnd-app/frontend/styles.css (current styles)
- ~/dnd-app/frontend/guide.html (content to merge)
- ~/dnd-app/frontend/flow.html (content to merge)
- ~/dnd-app/backend/server.js (all endpoints)
- ~/dnd-app/backend/bestiary-data.js
- ~/dnd-app/backend/spells-data.js

## Output Required

### 1. New App Architecture
- Single index.html structure
- Bottom tab bar with 4 tabs
- Panel system (slide-in panels for sub-features)
- How guide/flow content gets merged in

### 2. AI Assistant Spec
- Backend endpoint: POST /api/rooms/:pin/ai-chat
- System prompt (include session context)
- How context is gathered (party, combat, notes, rolls)
- Frontend chat UI
- Suggested prompts
- History management

### 3. UI Component Inventory
Every UI element needed, with exact layout:
- Dice roller (button sizes, animation, result display)
- Initiative tracker (simple mode)
- Character sheet (simplified for mobile)
- HP tracker (tap to change)
- Spell lookup (search + filter)
- Monster reference
- AI chat panel
- More menu grid

### 4. Onboarding Flow
Step-by-step for DM, Player, Solo — with exact UI

### 5. CSS/Design System
- Color tokens (amber variant of Apex)
- Typography (Cinzel for titles, JetBrains Mono for stats)
- Component styles
- Animations (dice roll, panel slide, HP change)

### 6. 4-Agent Execution Plan
- Agent 1: Backend (AI chat endpoint, merge APIs)
- Agent 2: Frontend layout (index.html rewrite, tab system, panels)
- Agent 3: Play + Party tabs (dice, initiative, character sheets)
- Agent 4: AI tab + More tab (chat UI, spell/monster/guide/settings)

### 7. Migration Plan
- How to merge guide.html content into index.html
- How to merge flow.html content
- What to keep from current index.html vs rewrite
- Backward compatibility (existing PINs/rooms still work?)

### 8. Mobile-First Responsive
- Bottom tab bar: fixed at bottom, 64px height
- Touch targets: minimum 48px
- Swipe between tabs?
- Full-screen panels slide from right
- No horizontal scrolling anywhere
I'll start by reading all the source files to understand the current codebase thoroughly before producing the plan.

Adventurer's Guide

Everything you need to know to run and play in your D&D 2024 sessions using Paul Simulator.

📜 Contents

  • 🏰 Getting Started
  • 👑 For the DM / Game Master
  • ⚔️ For Players
  • 💡 Tips & Tricks
  • ❓ Frequently Asked Questions
🏰

Getting Started

Paul Simulator is a real-time companion app for tabletop D&D 2024 sessions. It handles character sheets, dice rolling, initiative tracking, spell management, and session notes — so you can focus on the adventure.

No accounts needed. No downloads. Just open the link in your browser and jump in.

🌐 How to Access
Open the app URL in any modern browser. Desktop is recommended for the best experience, but mobile is fully supported. Works on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge.

How it works: The DM creates a room and gets a 6-digit PIN. Players each get a 4-digit PIN. Everyone enters their PIN to join the same room in real-time. Dice rolls, initiative, and character updates sync instantly across all connected devices.

📜 Character Sheets 🎲 Shared Dice Roller ⚔️ Initiative Tracker ✨ Spell Book 📝 Session Notes 🤖 AI Summaries 📓 Obsidian Export 🔄 Real-Time Sync
👑

For the DM / Game Master

As the DM, you control the room, manage players, track initiative, and record the session. Here's how to get everything set up.

🛠️ Setting Up Your Room

  1. Open the App & Create a Room
    Open the app URL in your browser. On the login screen, click "New DM? Create Room" at the bottom.
  2. Enter Your DM Name
    Type your name (e.g. "DM Paul") and click the "⚔️ Create Room" button.
  3. Save Your 6-Digit DM PIN
    You'll see your unique 6-digit DM PIN on screen. This is your login — save it somewhere safe! You can click 📋 to copy it.
    ⚠️ Don't Lose Your PIN!
    Your DM PIN is the only way to log back into your room. Write it down or save it in your notes.
  4. Add Your Players
    In the "Add Players" section, type each player's name and click "+ Add". Each player automatically receives a unique 4-digit PIN.
  5. Share the PINs
    Click "📋 Copy All PINs" to copy a formatted list of all player PINs. Paste it into your WhatsApp group, read them aloud, or send them however you like.
  6. Enter Your Dashboard
    Click "🏰 Enter as DM" to enter the main app. You're ready to run your session!

🎮 DM Tools & Features

  1. 👥 Party Overview
    Your default view. See all players' characters at a glance — their name, class, level, HP, AC, and ability scores. Click any character card for a detailed read-only view. Hit "🔄 Refresh" to pull the latest updates.
  2. 🎯 Manage Players
    Add or remove players at any time. View everyone's PINs, regenerate a PIN if someone needs a new one (🔄), or remove a player (❌). Your DM PIN is also displayed here.
  3. ⚔️ Initiative Tracker
    Click "+ Add Combatant" to add monsters/NPCs with their initiative, HP, and DEX modifier. Use "+ Add All Players" to pull in the whole party at once. Click "🎲 Roll Initiative" to auto-roll for a combatant, or enter it manually. Use "Next Turn ▶" to advance through the round. The active combatant is highlighted for everyone in the room.
  4. 🎲 Dice Roller
    DMs have full access to the dice roller too. Roll for NPCs, monsters, or secret checks. All rolls are visible to everyone in the room in real-time.
  5. 📝 Session Notes
    Write notes as you play — key events, NPC interactions, player decisions. Notes auto-save every 30 seconds, or click "💾 Save Notes" to save manually.
  6. 🤖 AI Summary
    Click "🤖 AI Summary" to auto-generate a narrative recap of your session based on your notes. Great for "previously on..." moments at the start of next session.
  7. 📓 Save to Obsidian
    Export your session notes and summary directly to your Obsidian vault. Click "📓 Save to Obsidian" and it's saved as a beautifully formatted note.
  8. 🔚 End Session
    When the session is over, click "🔚 End Session". This wraps up the session, archives everything, and saves it to your session history for future reference.
⚔️

For Players

As a player, you manage your character, roll dice, cast spells, and follow initiative. Here's how to get into the game and make the most of every feature.

🚪 Joining the Game

  1. Enter Your PIN
    Open the app URL. On the login screen, type the 4-digit PIN your DM gave you and click "⚔️ Enter Game". That's it — you're in!

📜 Character Sheet

Your character sheet is where you build and manage your hero. Everything auto-calculates as you make choices.

  1. Create a New Character
    Click "+ New Character" to start fresh. Give your character a heroic name and set their level.
  2. Choose Species, Class & Background
    Pick from the D&D 2024 options using the dropdown menus:
    • 10 Species: Human, Elf, Dwarf, Halfling, Orc, Tiefling, Dragonborn, Gnome, Goliath, Aasimar
    • 12 Classes: Barbarian, Bard, Cleric, Druid, Fighter, Monk, Paladin, Ranger, Rogue, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard
    • 16 Backgrounds: Acolyte, Artisan, Charlatan, Criminal, Entertainer, Farmer, Guard, Guide, Hermit, Merchant, Noble, Sage, Sailor, Scribe, Soldier, Wayfarer
  3. Set Ability Scores
    Choose your method from the dropdown:
    • Standard Array — assign 15, 14, 13, 12, 10, 8 to your abilities
    • Point Buy (27 pts) — use +/- buttons to distribute points (scores 8–15)
    • Manual Entry — type in any values (for rolled stats or homebrew)
    Then choose your Background Ability Bonus: +2 to one ability and +1 to another.
  4. Everything Auto-Calculates
    Once you've made your choices, the sheet handles the math:
    • HP — based on class hit die + CON modifier
    • AC — 10 + DEX modifier (unarmored base)
    • Initiative — DEX modifier
    • Proficiency Bonus — based on level
    • Saving Throws — auto-set by class, with proficiency applied
    • Skills — check the skills you're proficient in
    • Spell Slots — auto-calculated for spellcasters based on class and level
  5. Character Notes & Auto-Save
    Use the notes section for equipment, features, backstory, or anything else. Your character auto-saves as you make changes — no save button needed.

🎲 Dice Roller

The dice roller is shared across the room — everyone sees everyone's rolls in real-time!

  1. Pick a Die & Roll
    Click any die shape to select it: d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20, d100. Then click "🎲 Roll!". The result appears in the dice tray with a satisfying animation.
  2. Multi-Dice & Special Rolls
    Use the quick buttons for common rolls:
    • 2d6, 3d6, 4d6 — roll multiple dice at once
    • 4d6 drop low — roll 4d6 and drop the lowest (for ability score generation)
    • Advantage — roll 2d20, take the higher
    • Disadvantage — roll 2d20, take the lower
  3. Add Modifiers
    Use the Modifier dropdown to add +1 to +10 (or -1 to -5) to your roll. Perfect for attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws.
  4. Quick Roll Presets
    One-click presets for common situations: ⚔️ Attack, 🎯 Ability Check, 🛡️ Saving Throw, 🗡️ Damage rolls (various dice), 💚 Healing rolls.
  5. Custom Notation
    Type any dice notation in the custom field: 2d6+3, 4d8-1, 1d20+5. Choose Normal, Advantage, or Disadvantage mode, then roll.
👀 Shared Rolls
All dice rolls are broadcast to everyone in the room. The DM and all other players can see your rolls in the Roll History — no hiding bad rolls! 😄

✨ Spell Book

  1. Browse & Search Spells
    Use the search bar to find spells by name. Filter by spell level (Cantrips through 3rd) and class (Bard, Cleric, Druid, Paladin, Ranger, Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard).
  2. Prepare Spells
    Click a spell to see its details, then mark it as Prepared. Toggle "Prepared Only" to filter your list down to just the spells you have ready.
  3. Track Spell Slots
    The Spell Slot Tracker at the bottom shows your available slots by level. Check off boxes as you expend slots during the session. Slots are also shown on your Character Sheet.

⚔️ Initiative

  1. See the Combat Order
    During combat, the Initiative Tracker shows all combatants sorted by initiative. The active combatant is highlighted so everyone knows whose turn it is. Updates happen in real-time — when the DM advances the turn, you see it instantly.
💡

Tips & Tricks

🖥️
Use Desktop
Desktop gives you the full sidebar + wide layout. Mobile works, but desktop is the best experience.
🔑
Save Your PIN
Your PIN is your login. Save it in your notes, take a screenshot, or pin it in your WhatsApp chat. Don't lose it!
👥
DM: Party Overview for Roleplay
Keep Party Overview open during roleplay to reference characters. Switch to Initiative when combat starts.
📋
Copy All PINs
Use "Copy All PINs" to share a formatted PIN list. Paste it into your WhatsApp group — done in seconds.
✨
Nat 20 = Gold Glow!
Rolling a natural 20 triggers a golden CRITICAL effect. Nat 1 gives you a red FUMBLE glow. The drama is real!
💾
Auto-Save Everything
Character sheets auto-save on every change. Session notes auto-save every 30 seconds. Your data is safe.
📱
Mobile Players
Players on phones: use the ☰ hamburger menu to navigate between Character, Dice, Spells, and Initiative tabs.
🔄
Real-Time Sync
Dice rolls, initiative changes, and character updates sync instantly via WebSocket. No need to refresh.
❓

Frequently Asked Questions

🔑 What if I forget my PIN? ▶
Ask your DM! They can see all player PINs in the Manage Players tab. The DM can also regenerate a new PIN for you if needed.
📱 Can I use this on my phone? ▶
Yes! The app is fully responsive and works on mobile browsers. That said, desktop is recommended for the best experience — especially for DMs managing a lot of features at once.
💾 Do I lose my character if I close the browser? ▶
No! Your character data is saved on the server and also backed up in localStorage. Just log back in with your PIN and everything will be there.
👀 Can other players see my character sheet? ▶
Only the DM can see all characters through the Party Overview (read-only). Other players cannot view your character sheet. Your secrets are safe... mostly.
🎲 Can other players see my dice rolls? ▶
Yes! All dice rolls are shared with the entire room in real-time. This keeps the game transparent and honest — no fudging! The DM and all players see your rolls in the Roll History.
🔄 What if I get disconnected? ▶
The app automatically reconnects via WebSocket every 3 seconds. If you lose connection briefly, you'll be back in the room moments later. Your data is saved on the server, so nothing is lost.
🧙 Can I have multiple characters? ▶
Yes! Click "+ New Character" to create additional characters. Use the dropdown at the top of the Character Sheet to switch between them. You can also delete characters you no longer need.
📓 What is "Save to Obsidian"? ▶
Obsidian is a popular note-taking app. The DM can export session notes and AI summaries directly into their Obsidian vault for long-term campaign documentation. It's optional — only useful if you use Obsidian.
🐉

  • Freeform notes during the session — auto-saves every 30 seconds

// bestiary-data.js — Pre-loaded D&D 5e/2024 Monster Bestiary (~50 monsters)

# ULTRAPLAN: D&D Paul Simulator — TOTAL UI/UX REMODEL + AI Assistant **Author:** Michael (ULTRAPLAN subagent) **Date:** 2026-04-05 **Status:** PLAN — Do NOT execute until Lucy approves **Philosophy:** K.I.S.S. — "aku ingin orang bodoh pun paham pake aplikasi ini" --- ## Table of Contents 1. [Executive Summary](#1-executive-summary) 2. [New App Architecture](#2-new-app-architecture) 3. [AI Assistant Specification](#3-ai-assistant-specification) 4. [UI Component Inventory](#4-ui-component-inventory) 5. [Onboarding Flow](#5-onboarding-flow) 6. [CSS / Design System](#6-css--design-system) 7. [4-Agent Execution Plan](#7-4-agent-execution-plan) 8. [Migration Plan](#8-migration-plan) 9. [Mobile-First Responsive](#9-mobile-first-responsive) 10. [Risk Assessment](#10-risk-assessment) --- ## 1. Executive Summary ### What We're Doing Transforming a 3-page, 9-sidebar-nav D&D companion app into a single-page, 4-tab, mobile-first app with an integrated AI assistant. Merging `guide.html` and `flow.html` into `index.html`. Replacing the sidebar nav with a bottom tab bar. ### Key Decisions | Decision | Choice | Why | |----------|--------|-----| | Navigation | Bottom tab bar (4 tabs) | Mobile thumb-friendly, K.I.S.S. | | AI Integration | New chat panel + backend endpoint | Uses existing Bedrock via Council | | Pages | Single `index.html` | Zero page switches | | Sub-features | Slide-in panels (not pages, not modals) | Full-screen feel, back button | | Design | Apex Dark + Amber (refined) | Already partially done, D&D fantasy feel | | CSS | Complete rewrite of `styles.css` | Current 6130 lines is unmaintainable | ### What Gets Killed - `guide.html` → content becomes contextual help tooltips + "Guide" panel in More tab - `flow.html` → content is purely documentation, not user-facing; archive it - Left sidebar nav (desktop) → replaced by bottom tab bar on all screens - MAGI sidebar (Apex sidebar from parent app) → removed from D&D pages - 9 sidebar nav items → 4 bottom tabs - Spectator mode → moves to More tab (low priority feature) ### What Gets Added - **AI Assistant tab** (chat with session context) - **Bottom tab bar** (Play, Party, AI, More) - **Slide-in panel system** (for sub-features) - **Solo Adventure** flow (AI DM, already partially built) - **Contextual help** (? icons with tooltips) --- ## 2. New App Architecture ### 2.1 Single `index.html` Structure ``` index.html ├── (fonts, styles.css) ├── │ ├── #login-screen (onboarding — visible when not authenticated) │ │ ├── .onboard-hero (dragon + title) │ │ ├── .onboard-buttons (3 big buttons: Create Game, Join Game, Solo) │ │ ├── #create-flow (step-by-step DM creation) │ │ ├── #join-flow (PIN entry for players) │ │ └── #solo-flow (character select → genre → go) │ │ │ ├── #app-screen (main app — visible after auth) │ │ ├── #app-header (room info bar — PIN, name, role) │ │ ├── #tab-content (main content area) │ │ │ ├── #tab-play (dice + initiative + quick actions) │ │ │ ├── #tab-party (party overview + character sheets) │ │ │ ├── #tab-ai (AI assistant chat) │ │ │ └── #tab-more (grid of feature cards) │ │ ├── #panel-container (slide-in panels from More) │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="spells"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="monsters"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="npcs"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="map"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="notes"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="guide"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="history"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="settings"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="wiki"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="encounters"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="arena"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="journal"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="relationships"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="compendium"] │ │ │ ├── .panel[data-panel="rulebook"] │ │ │ └── .panel[data-panel="chat"] (in-game text chat) │ │ └── #bottom-tabs (fixed tab bar) │ │ │ └── ``` ### 2.2 Bottom Tab Bar ```html
``` ### 2.3 Panel System Panels slide in from the right, covering the full screen. Each panel has: - A header with title + back button (`← Back`) - Full-screen content area - Panels stack: opening a panel from within a panel pushes to stack ```javascript // Panel management const panelStack = []; function openPanel(panelId) { const panel = document.querySelector(`.panel[data-panel="${panelId}"]`); panel.classList.add('open'); panelStack.push(panelId); document.getElementById('panel-container').classList.add('has-panel'); } function closePanel() { const current = panelStack.pop(); document.querySelector(`.panel[data-panel="${current}"]`).classList.remove('open'); if (panelStack.length === 0) { document.getElementById('panel-container').classList.remove('has-panel'); } } function closeAllPanels() { panelStack.forEach(id => { document.querySelector(`.panel[data-panel="${id}"]`).classList.remove('open'); }); panelStack.length = 0; document.getElementById('panel-container').classList.remove('has-panel'); } ``` ### 2.4 How Guide Content Gets Merged The guide has 5 sections: Getting Started, For DM, For Players, Tips, FAQ. **Approach:** DON'T dump all guide text into the app. Instead: 1. **Contextual tooltips** — Small `?` icons next to complex features. Tap → shows a tooltip/popover with the relevant guide snippet. 2. **Guide panel** in More tab — A searchable, collapsible guide for people who want to read everything. Uses the same content from `guide.html` but rendered as collapsible accordions inside a slide-in panel. 3. **Onboarding hints** — First-time DM/player sees 3-step overlay ("Tap dice to roll", "Your party is here", etc.) — dismissable, never shown again (stored in localStorage). **Content mapping:** | Guide Section | New Location | |---------------|-------------| | Getting Started | Onboarding flow (built into login screen) | | For the DM | Tooltips on DM-only features + Guide panel | | For Players | Tooltips on player features + Guide panel | | Tips & Tricks | Guide panel "Tips" section | | FAQ | Guide panel "FAQ" section (collapsible) | ### 2.5 How Flow Content Gets Merged `flow.html` is developer/architecture documentation. It shows the data flow, access matrix, and tech stack. **Approach:** This is NOT user-facing. Don't merge it into the app UI. - **Archive** `flow.html` to `docs/flow.html` (or keep it where it is) - The access matrix info is useful internally — encode it as JS constants in `app.js`: ```javascript const ROLE_PERMISSIONS = { dm: ['party', 'manage', 'initiative-control', 'notes', 'ai-summary', 'npcs', 'monsters', 'encounters', 'wiki-edit', 'map-edit', 'quest-gen', 'trap-gen'], player: ['character-edit-own', 'dice', 'initiative-view', 'spells', 'journal', 'ai-chat'], spectator: ['initiative-view', 'dice-view'] }; ``` --- ## 3. AI Assistant Specification ### 3.1 Backend Endpoint **New endpoint:** `POST /api/rooms/:pin/ai-chat` ```javascript // server.js — new endpoint app.post('/api/rooms/:pin/ai-chat', async (req, res) => { const { message, conversationHistory, userId, role } = req.body; if (!message) return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Message required' }); const rooms = readJSON('rooms.json'); const room = rooms[req.params.pin]; if (!room) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Room not found' }); // Gather session context const chars = readJSON('characters.json'); const roomChars = Object.values(chars).filter(c => c.roomPin === req.params.pin); const context = buildSessionContext(room, roomChars); // Build conversation for AI const systemPrompt = buildAISystemPrompt(context, role); const recentHistory = (conversationHistory || []).slice(-20).map(m => `${m.role === 'user' ? 'User' : 'AI'}: ${m.text}` ).join('\n'); const aiPrompt = `${systemPrompt}\n\nConversation so far:\n${recentHistory}\n\nUser: ${message}\n\nRespond helpfully. Keep answers concise (under 300 words unless detailed rules explanation needed). Use markdown formatting.`; try { const response = await fetch('http://127.0.0.1:3001/voice/chat', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ agentId: 'sarael', message: aiPrompt }) }); const data = await response.json(); const reply = data.response || data.message || 'The oracle is silent...'; // Save to room's AI chat history if (!room.aiChatHistory) room.aiChatHistory = []; room.aiChatHistory.push( { role: 'user', text: message, userId, timestamp: Date.now() }, { role: 'ai', text: reply, timestamp: Date.now() } ); // Keep last 100 messages if (room.aiChatHistory.length > 100) { room.aiChatHistory = room.aiChatHistory.slice(-100); } rooms[req.params.pin] = room; writeJSON('rooms.json', rooms); res.json({ reply, context: { partySize: roomChars.length, combatActive: room.initiative?.active || false } }); } catch (err) { res.status(500).json({ error: 'AI service unavailable: ' + err.message }); } }); // Get AI chat history for a room app.get('/api/rooms/:pin/ai-chat', (req, res) => { const rooms = readJSON('rooms.json'); const room = rooms[req.params.pin]; if (!room) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Room not found' }); res.json(room.aiChatHistory || []); }); ``` ### 3.2 System Prompt (with Session Context) ```javascript function buildSessionContext(room, chars) { return { party: chars.map(c => ({ name: c.name, playerName: c.playerName, species: c.species, className: c.className, level: c.level, hp: c.currentHP, maxHp: calcMaxHP(c), ac: c.ac || 10, conditions: c.conditions || [] })), combat: { active: room.initiative?.active || false, currentTurn: room.initiative?.currentTurn || 0, combatants: (room.initiative?.combatants || []).map(c => ({ name: c.name, hp: c.currentHP, maxHp: c.maxHP, conditions: c.conditions || [], type: c.type })) }, recentRolls: (room.rollHistory || []).slice(-10).map(r => ({ player: r.userName, notation: r.notation, total: r.total, timestamp: r.timestamp })), sessionNotes: (room.sessionNotes || '').slice(-500), roomName: room.dmName + "'s Game" }; } function buildAISystemPrompt(context, role) { const partyList = context.party.map(c => ` - ${c.name} (${c.species} ${c.className} L${c.level}, HP: ${c.hp}/${c.maxHp}, AC: ${c.ac})` ).join('\n'); const combatInfo = context.combat.active ? `\nCOMBAT IS ACTIVE. Turn: ${context.combat.currentTurn}.\nCombatants:\n${ context.combat.combatants.map(c => ` - ${c.name}: HP ${c.hp}/${c.maxHp} [${c.type}]`).join('\n') }` : '\nNo active combat.'; const recentRolls = context.recentRolls.length > 0 ? `\nRecent rolls:\n${context.recentRolls.map(r => ` - ${r.player}: ${r.notation} → ${r.total}`).join('\n')}` : ''; return `You are a D&D 2024 rules expert and helpful assistant integrated into Paul Simulator, a D&D companion app. CURRENT SESSION CONTEXT: Game: ${context.roomName} User role: ${role === 'dm' ? 'Dungeon Master' : 'Player'} PARTY: ${partyList || ' No characters created yet.'} ${combatInfo} ${recentRolls} SESSION NOTES (last 500 chars): ${context.sessionNotes || 'No notes yet.'} RULES: - Answer D&D 2024 rules questions accurately - Suggest encounters appropriate for the party level - Help with NPC roleplay ideas - Suggest tactical options based on current combat state - If asked about specific characters, use the party data above - Be concise, fun, and helpful - Use markdown for formatting (bold, lists, headers) - If unsure about a rule, say so rather than making it up`; } ``` ### 3.3 Frontend Chat UI ```html

Build More tab grid (Section 4.4) 11. Wire up existing JS app logic to new DOM structure (ensure `app.js` references are updated) **Files modified:** - `~/dnd-app/frontend/index.html` (full rewrite) - `~/dnd-app/frontend/styles.css` (full rewrite) **Files archived:** - `~/dnd-app/frontend/guide.html` → `~/dnd-app/docs/guide.html` - `~/dnd-app/frontend/flow.html` → `~/dnd-app/docs/flow.html` **Estimated time:** 30-40 minutes **Acceptance criteria:** - Single `index.html` loads with bottom tab bar - Tab switching works (Play, Party, AI, More) - Panel system opens/closes correctly - Login flow works for DM and Player - More tab shows correct cards based on role - No horizontal scrolling on any screen - Responsive from 320px to 1440px **CRITICAL DEPENDENCIES:** - Must coordinate with Agent 3 and Agent 4 on DOM element IDs - Must keep all existing IDs that `app.js` references, OR update `app.js` --- ### Agent 3: Play Tab + Party Tab (dice, initiative, character sheets) **Scope:** Build the two most-used tabs. Mobile-First Responsive ### 9.1 Bottom Tab Bar ```css #bottom-tabs { position: fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0; height: 64px; z-index: 200; /* Safe area for iPhone notch */ padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0); } ``` ### 9.2 Touch Targets - **Minimum touch target: 48×48px** (Google Material Design guideline) - Dice buttons: 64×64px ✅ - Tab bar buttons: full width of tab × 64px ✅ - More-tab feature cards: min 80×80px ✅ - Initiative list items: 48px min-height ✅ - All buttons: min 44px height ✅ ### 9.3 Swipe Between Tabs? **Decision: NO.** Reason: Swipe conflicts with panel slide-in and horizontal scroll areas (spell list, dice presets). Swipe gestures add complexity and can be accidentally triggered. Tabs are always visible — just tap. Exception: Swipe-right on a panel to close it (natural "back" gesture). This is safe because panels are full-screen overlays. ```css .panel { /* Touch gesture: swipe right to close */ touch-action: pan-y; } ``` ### 9.4 Full-Screen Panels ```css .panel { position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; bottom: 0; z-index: 100; background: var(--bg-base); overflow-y: auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch; transform: translateX(100%); transition: transform 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .panel.open { transform: translateX(0); } .panel-header { position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 10; background: rgba(13, 18, 27, 0.95); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); padding: 12px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border); display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; } .panel-back-btn { min-width: 48px; min-height: 48px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: none; border: none; color: var(--gold); font-size: 1.2rem; cursor: pointer; } .panel-body { padding: 16px; padding-bottom: calc(var(--tab-height) + 16px); /* Space for tab bar */ } ``` ### 9.5 No Horizontal Scrolling - All grids: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(140px, 1fr))` - All tables: replaced with card lists on mobile - Form rows: `flex-wrap: wrap` - Ability scores: 2×3 grid on mobile (not 6-across) - Text inputs: `width: 100%`, never side-by-side on mobile unless very short - Overflow-x: hidden on body ### 9.6 Viewport Breakpoints ```css /* Mobile first — base styles are mobile */ /* Small phones */ @media (min-width: 375px) { /* iPhone SE+ */ } /* Standard phones */ @media (min-width: 414px) { /* iPhone Plus+ */ } /* Tablets */ @media (min-width: 768px) { /* Two-column layouts where appropriate */ /* Dice roller: dice + history side by side */ /* Party: cards in 2-column grid */ } /* Desktop */ @media (min-width: 1024px) { /* Three-column where appropriate */ /* Party: cards in 3-column grid */ /* More tab: 3-column grid */ /* Optional: replace bottom tab bar with side rail? NO — keep consistent */ } ``` --- ## 10. Create ID migration table. | | AI endpoint overwhelms Bedrock | Cost spike | Rate limit: max 1 request per 3 seconds per room. Test on 320px, 375px, 768px, 1440px. | ### Medium Risk | Risk | Impact | Mitigation | |------|--------|------------| | WebSocket events don't fire in new DOM | Real-time breaks | WebSocket handler code stays in `app.js`; just update DOM selectors. | | Character sheet mobile layout too cramped | Bad UX | Use collapsible sections. by `#bottom-tabs` | | `#main-content` | `#tab-content` | Renamed | | `#tab-dice` | Inside `#tab-play` | Merged into Play tab | | `#tab-initiative` | Inside `#tab-play` | Merged into Play tab | | `#tab-party` | `#tab-party` | Same | | `#tab-character` | Panel: `[data-panel="character"]` | Now a panel from Party tab | | `#tab-spells` | Panel: `[data-panel="spells"]` | Now a panel from More tab | | `#tab-monsters` | Panel: `[data-panel="monsters"]` | Now a panel from More tab | | `#tab-npcs` | Panel: `[data-panel="npcs"]` | Now a panel from More tab | | `#tab-map` | Panel: | | `#tab-compendium` | Panel: `[data-panel="compendium"]` | Now a panel from More tab | | `#tab-rulebook` | Panel: `[data-panel="rulebook"]` | Now a panel from More tab | | `#tab-encounters` | Panel: ## ULTRAPLAN Complete ✅

I've read all 7 source files (index.html, styles.css, guide.html, flow.html, server.js, bestiary-data.js, spells-data.js) and produced a comprehensive plan at `~/dnd-app/ULTRAPLAN-REMODEL.md`.

### What the Plan Covers:

1. **New App Architecture** — Single `index.html` with bottom tab bar (Play, Party, AI, More), panel system for sub-features, removal of MAGI sidebar
2. **AI Assistant Spec** — Full `POST /api/rooms/:pin/ai-chat` endpoint with session context gathering (party, combat, rolls, notes), system prompt template, frontend chat UI with context-aware suggested prompts, history management (100 messages per room)
3. **UI Component Inventory** — Exact layouts for every screen: dice roller (64px buttons, tap-to-roll), initiative tracker (compact mode), party cards with HP bars, character sheet (mobile 2×3 ability grid), More tab (2-column feature card grid with 16 panels)
4. **Onboarding Flow** — 3 big buttons (Create Game, Join Game, Solo Adventure), step-by-step DM/Player/Solo flows with exact UI mockups
5. **CSS/Design System** — Complete color tokens (Apex Dark + Amber), typography (Cinzel + JetBrains Mono), 6 key animations (dice reveal, crit glow, panel slide, HP flash), bottom tab bar styling
6. **4-Agent Execution Plan** with dependencies:
   - **Agent 1 (Backend):** AI chat endpoint + helpers (~15-20 min)
   - **Agent 2 (Frontend Layout):** Full HTML/CSS rewrite, tab system, panels (~30-40 min)
   - **Agent 3 (Play + Party):** Dice roller, initiative, character sheets (~30-40 min)
   - **Agent 4 (AI + More):** Chat UI, guide panel, all feature panels (~35-45 min)
7. **Migration Plan** — DOM ID migration table mapping all 20+ current IDs to new locations, guide.html content → accordion panels + tooltips, flow.html → archived
8. **Mobile-First Responsive** — 48px min touch targets, safe area insets, no horizontal scroll, breakpoints at 375/414/768/1024px
9. **Risk Assessment** — High/medium/low risks with mitigations, including the critical `app.js` DOM reference breakage risk

### Key Decisions Made:
- **No swipe between tabs** (conflicts with panels)
- **Guide content becomes contextual tooltips + accordion panel** (not raw dump)
- **Flow.html archived** (developer docs, not user-facing)
- **AI rate limit:** 1 request per 3 seconds per room
- **Panel stack max depth:** 2 (avoid navigation confusion)
- **All existing endpoints and data preserved** (100% backward compatible)
